_comment: SYNTHETIC code (legacy) graph for the theory-eval harness. Conforms to schemas/code-graph.schema.json (applications -> {path, nodes, edges}; node.type in program|table|...; edge.type in call|read_write|...). Designed to exercise T1 (capability grouping) and T2 (entity co-location).

_design.PROG_A_CALLS_PROG_B: PROG_A calls PROG_B -> in functional mode PROG_B (single caller) is leaf-merged INTO PROG_A, forming exactly ONE capability node (CAP_*) from two programs. In structural mode they stay as 2 separate REQ_ nodes. This is what EVAL_T1 checks (1 merged cap vs 2 reqs).
_design.SHARED_ASSET: Both PROG_A and PROG_B read_write the CUSTOMER table -> they share a data asset, so they belong in the same capability/domain. PROG_A also accesses CONFIG and LEDGER, so its requirement touches THREE assets that must all co-locate into its domain (EVAL_T2 subset invariant).
_design.ISOLATED_PROG_C: PROG_C has no callers and accesses only the ARCHIVE table -> an isolated program in its own asset domain. Present so the harness has at least one standalone program / standalone asset (entity-only-domain candidate that functional-mode suppression should fold away).

details.note: Entry program; calls PROG_B; accesses CUSTOMER, CONFIG, LEDGER
Called only by PROG_A (single caller -> leaf-merge target); accesses CUSTOMER
Isolated program; no callers; accesses ARCHIVE only
Shared data asset between PROG_A and PROG_B
